figures.caption: Figure 1: Schematic of the flexible beam (gray shaded region) clamped to a rigid cylinder (white circle) and immersed in a channel flow.

Figure 7: Comparison of prediction and truth of flow field at time 𝑡2 for 𝑐2=-2: (a) 𝑢 𝑓 , (b) 𝑣 𝑓 , (c) 𝑝 𝑓 .

Figure 5: Comparison of prediction and truth of flow field 𝑢 𝑓 on non-periodic regime for 𝑐2=-2 at time (a) 𝑡1, (b) 𝑡2, (c) 𝑡3.

Figure 2: ALE-consistent GNO–Transformer framework for long-horizon FSI prediction. (a) Overview of the autoregressive rollout, where the fluid surrogate (GNO–ViT) and the structure surrogate (LSTM) are advanced in a staggered manner and supervised by step-wise losses to suppress error accumulation. (b) Detailed schematic of one rollout cycle with red circled indices (1-6) corresponding to the six major steps in Algorithm 1: Initialization, Mesh update, Fluid prediction, ALE-consistent boundary correction, Pressure transfer to structure and Structure prediction. Dashed arrows indicate the coupling information exchanged between the two surrogates, and loss𝑡 𝑓 and loss𝑡𝑠 denote the step-wise training losses used in the two-stage training protocol.
Figure 11: Time-evolution of predicted 𝑝 𝑓 errors by different method for 𝑐2 = −2: (a) GNO-ViT, (b) GNO, (c) GNO-ViT-noBM.

Figure 9: Time evolution of the correlation coefficient 𝑅2 during rollouts for (a) velocity field 𝑢 𝑓 and (b) the pressure field 𝑝 𝑓 , comparing GNO-ViT, GNO-ViT-noBM, and the GNO. The inset in (a) highlights the early-stage of 𝑢 𝑓 .
Figure 10: Comparison of boundary pressure predictions by GNO-ViT, GNO and GNO-ViT-NoBM for 𝑐2 = −2: (a) GNO-ViT, (b) GNO and GNO-ViT-NoBM.

Figure A.4: Comparison of prediction and truth of flow field at time 𝑡2 for 𝑐2=6: (a) 𝑢 𝑓 , (b) 𝑣 𝑓 , (c) 𝑝 𝑓 .
Figure 15: Time evolution of the flow-field correlation coefficient 𝑅2 of 𝑢 𝑓 between prediction and truth for short-term and long-term training: (a) 𝑐2 = −2; (b) 𝑐2 = 6.

Figure 14: Prediction and truth for the time histories of normalized structural displacements at monitoring point A in the periodic regime using short-term training only (without long-term fine-tuning): (a) 𝑥/𝑑 for 𝑐2 = −2; (b) 𝑦/𝑑 for 𝑐2 = −2; (c) 𝑥/𝑑 for 𝑐2 = 6; (d) 𝑦/𝑑 for 𝑐2 = 6.
Figure 8: Truth and prediction results of flow field by different methods at step 1 and step 15 for 𝑐2=-2: (a) truth, (b) GNO-ViT, (c) GNO, (d) GNO-ViT-noBM.
